title: resume-site Configuration
description: JSON Schema for config.yaml — infrastructure settings only.

secret_key.description: Flask secret key for sessions and CSRF. Must be at least 32 characters for production.

database_path.description: Path to the SQLite database file.

max_upload_size.description: Maximum file upload size in bytes (default 10 MB).

session_timeout_minutes.description: Admin session inactivity timeout in minutes.

session_cookie_secure.description: Set the Secure flag on the session cookie (HTTPS-only). Disable for plain-HTTP local development.

trusted_proxies.description: Phase 22.6: CIDR ranges whose inbound connections may legitimately carry an X-Forwarded-For header. The admin IP allowlist consults XFF only when request.remote_addr falls inside this list; otherwise XFF is ignored and remote_addr is used directly. Phase 23.2 extends the same trust model to every IP-trusting callsite via get_client_ip().

canonical_host.description: Phase 23.5 (#57): optional canonical origin (e.g. 'https://example.com') used when building absolute URLs in /sitemap.xml, /robots.txt, and /blog/feed.xml. When unset, falls back to request.url_root (the pre-23.5 behaviour) — set this on any deployment where the origin is reachable under multiple Host headers to prevent a spoofed Host from poisoning cached SEO artifacts and RSS subscriptions.

max_request_size.description: Phase 23.5 (#37): Flask MAX_CONTENT_LENGTH cap (bytes) for the total request body. Werkzeug rejects with 413 before any view code runs when Content-Length exceeds this. Default 16 MiB covers a 10 MB photo upload plus form overhead; raise only if a specific upload path needs more.

password_file.description: Path to file containing SMTP password (Docker/Podman secrets).

from_address.description: Optional. Address used in the From header of outbound mail. Falls back to `user` when unset. Needed for relay providers (Resend, SendGrid, Mailgun) where the SMTP login identity differs from the verified sender address.

recipient.description: Email address for contact form submissions.

allowed_networks.description: CIDR ranges allowed to access admin routes.
